115912.explanation: In Ambacht, we use a form of participation that functions very well.

115913.explanation: If this is a good solution, then this is an option. This already happens and produces positive results.

115915.explanation: The municipality sets the limits for opening hours, within which cafés and restaurants can choose their own opening times.

115917.explanation: Any potential establishment must be possible within the available space and fit the municipality.

115918.explanation: There is no asylum reception center, only emergency shelter at the moment. This is working well. We want to be hospitable to people fleeing war and persecution. But we are against an asylum seeker center (azc). We want to extend the emergency shelter on the water. This is going well now.

115922.explanation: This is an option, but not necessary. We first need to gain experience with the new situation where fireworks are banned.

115924.explanation: The current standards meet legal requirements, but it would be pleasant for residents if local standards were tightened.

115925.explanation: It is desirable that buildings are accessible for people with disabilities. But we see this as the primary responsibility of entrepreneurs. The municipality is responsible for public facilities.

115926.explanation: There are already many facilities for children from families on welfare benefits at this time.

115933.explanation: More targeted camera surveillance can contribute to safety and enforcement, but prevention is more important.

115934.explanation: As far as is known, there are sufficient facilities in the region.

115935.explanation: Enforcement officers may enforce rules, but do not have the same authority as the police. They do not need this baton. If it is nationally decided that it is wise to introduce it, we will not oppose it.

115938.explanation: We want to prevent the purchase of homes for investment purposes as much as possible.

115939.explanation: That is an option for certain business parks, but not for every business park.

115941.explanation: Yes, we find reciprocity important. Where possible, certainly invest in it.

115943.explanation: Preventing poverty is important for enabling people to participate in society and can prevent other problems, such as reliance on care.

115945.explanation: Desirable in combination with paying for parking too long in such a place. Promoting home charging stations also belongs here.

115946.explanation: 50 km/h where possible, 30 km/h where necessary.

115947.explanation: Parking permits only where they contribute to good parking facilities. Parking should remain free and easy.

115948.explanation: The problem does not seem relevant in Ambacht. National legislation is expected that will regulate this.

115949.explanation: We support freedom of choice in care. Care that best fits gives the best results.

115950.explanation: It is about the combination of quality and price.

115951.explanation: This is not a responsibility of the municipality.

115953.explanation: The dog tax is used for facilities for dogs and to counteract nuisance caused by dogs.

115954.explanation: At this moment there is already extensive surveillance on the Ambachtsezoom, so we agree with this statement. Further expansion does not seem necessary or desirable.

115959.explanation: As far as we are concerned, there will be no coffeeshop in Ambacht.

115962.explanation: This may be possible to a limited extent with clear rules.

115965.explanation: Events in our municipality are accessible to everyone, promote connection, fit the identity of the municipality, and respect Sunday rest. According to the SGP-ChristenUnie, there should therefore be no expansion of the number of events on Sundays.

115967.explanation: So far, this has led to very good waste separation and a substantial reduction of residual waste (which cannot be reused). This is also financially attractive for residents. We want to continue and keep stimulating this trend.

115912.explanation: Participation is an important pillar of our policy. We involve residents from the start on important issues, before decisions are made!

115913.explanation: There must be clear rules about the conditions under which this occurs. For green spaces, it is very important what design is intended so that quality and appearance are maintained.

115915.explanation: We do think that opening hours may be extended somewhat. Completely freeing them up carries the risk of nuisance.

115917.explanation: We already have several distribution centers in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht. More distribution centers will worsen accessibility due to more traffic movements from the highway. This will also negatively affect traffic safety.

115918.explanation: But as long as the dispersal law is in effect, we have no local say in this. We are opposed to an asylum seeker center anyway. Also because long-term shelter is financially unfeasible. Nationally, 7 billion will be cut from this between 2026 and 2029. The municipality should not commit to long-term obligations.

115922.explanation: If a fireworks ban is imposed, an alternative must be offered. Otherwise, this encourages illegal fireworks.

115924.explanation: So far, we have heard a minimal number of complaints about events. Clear noise standards have been established for each event.

115925.explanation: Ambacht’s entrepreneurs are very capable of arranging this themselves. A patronizing attitude from the municipality is not needed.

115926.explanation: There are already many schemes covering this, namely: Sports Fund, SMS children’s fund from the Leergeld foundation, collection points, and vouchers for sports clothing. We consider sports very important because it fosters social cohesion and contributes to a healthier life.

115933.explanation: But only at locations where it is necessary. We consider the safety of our residents of the utmost importance. When camera surveillance is necessary to tackle nuisance, this weighs heavily.

115934.explanation: According to standard figures, there should be 1 place per 10,000 inhabitants. Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht currently records 40 reports of domestic violence per year.

115935.explanation: We are not an immediate advocate, but given recent incidents, we see that tough intervention is unfortunately necessary. A baton can have a deterrent effect here.

115938.explanation: We face a shortage of housing in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht, so leaving a house empty is inappropriate. Vacant properties also pose a risk of squatting, which often causes nuisance for neighbors. Unless it concerns rental in the private sector, keeping the property available.

115939.explanation: Project developers can handle that very well themselves. We must facilitate zoning plans to make these conversions possible.

115941.explanation: It is important to properly involve people with social assistance benefits in society. This helps them maintain good social contacts and thereby increases their chances of finding a job.

115943.explanation: We already have an upward deviation in the existing regulations. The Municipality also always offers a listening ear for individual cases.

115945.explanation: Public parking spaces are necessary for every resident, regardless of the type of car. However, we want to cooperate in enabling cable ducts in the sidewalk to allow for private charging options.

115946.explanation: To promote traffic flow, we enforce higher speeds where possible and 30 km/h where necessary for traffic safety.

115947.explanation: There must be sufficient parking spaces anyway, in good balance with the number of homes. For people with disabilities, an exemption must be possible.

115948.explanation: We live in a prosperous municipality and there are few polluting vehicles in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht.

115949.explanation: These choices are already possible now. We want to keep it that way.

115950.explanation: There must be a good price-quality ratio. Cheap often means expensive.

115951.explanation: Other institutions already distribute these products. These are MUPs (Menstruation Product Distribution Points), found for example at the library and the food bank, in cooperation with the national poverty fund.

115953.explanation: We want to continue offering value for money to everyone. Dog tax should be a purpose tax. If abolished, we can no longer offer the facilities currently available for dog owners.

115954.explanation: There are already enough business parks. Through renovation and land consolidation, these business parks can be better organized.

115959.explanation: There is a coffeeshop in Zwijndrecht, so a coffeeshop in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht is not necessary.

115962.explanation: The municipality has no policy on this, so it is already allowed. However, it is important that shared housing does not cause nuisance for the neighborhood.

115965.explanation: But in the newly introduced events policy, this is already allowed.

115967.explanation: Under the current system, the polluter pays and this provides the fairest distribution per household. It also encourages proper waste separation. Proper sorting results in savings for those households.

115912.explanation: Residents of Ambacht must be able to be involved in important decisions. This can take the form of a referendum.

115913.explanation: It is important that buildings and outdoor spaces remain in order. Residents can contribute to this, provided it continues to meet standards.

115915.explanation: Entrepreneurs should have the freedom to decide when a café, restaurant or shop opens or closes its doors.

115917.explanation: The establishment of distribution centers must create employment and fit the local traffic situation and flow.

115918.explanation: There is no room for an asylum seeker center (azc) in Ambacht. The shelter on ships will end as soon as the legal obligation expires or the costs are no longer covered by the State.

115922.explanation: The municipality should enable a fireworks show but is not an event agency. We leave the organization and execution to a (local) entrepreneur.

115924.explanation: The existing standards are strict enough.

115925.explanation: It is the entrepreneur's responsibility to comply with accessibility standards.

115926.explanation: The municipality receives funds from the national government for this. It is not necessary to supplement this.

115933.explanation: Where necessary, we implement (temporary) camera surveillance. This increases the chance of apprehension and provides monitoring of risk locations.

115934.explanation: We have no indication that one is needed, but if it becomes clear there is a need, we support it.

115935.explanation: If the situation requires it, BOAs (special enforcement officers) must be equipped with, among other things, a baton. This is already possible.

115938.explanation: It is important that homes are occupied. Buying a house to subsequently rent it out can contribute to creating living space for starters and those moving within the market.

115939.explanation: It is up to the developer to create a suitable business case. This happens without subsidies.

115941.explanation: Social assistance should be short-term support to get back to work. If it takes a long time, it is fair to require a counter-performance.

115943.explanation: We believe this income threshold should actually be lowered. Raising it increases costs in other areas.

115945.explanation: Reserving parking spaces for electric charging unnecessarily increases parking pressure in residential areas. In Ambacht, this pressure is already too high; therefore, no more public parking spaces should be reserved for charging electric cars.

115946.explanation: Traffic must flow smoothly: within the built-up area, 50 km/h remains the standard. Where possible, such as on the Ambachtsezoom, 70 km/h is being considered. In vulnerable situations, such as around schools, 30 km/h or lower remains in effect.

115947.explanation: Everyone wants to park close to home. Where parking pressure is too high, solutions like parking permits will be considered to reduce it.

115948.explanation: We are against zero-emission zones that restrict residents and entrepreneurs. Everyone must be able to reach their home or business by car.

115949.explanation: Healthcare costs are becoming too high. Choosing a care provider that matches faith, culture or beliefs is a luxury. It is unfair to pass those costs onto the community.

115950.explanation: Youth care must return to its essence to control costs. Care is for those who really need it; ordinary upbringing questions should not fall directly under it. Growing up involves trial and error. There is a clear responsibility here for parents and caregivers.

115951.explanation: This is a personal responsibility. Initiatives are already available for those with minimum incomes.

115953.explanation: In recent years, the VVD has halved the dog tax. Now we will abolish it completely.

115954.explanation: In Ambacht, we promote entrepreneurship and business activity.

115959.explanation: This should be possible, provided it concerns a location where residents do not experience any nuisance.

115962.explanation: It is unclear whether this makes sense and who would benefit from it.

115965.explanation: Ambacht will become a festival-friendly municipality. It will remain possible to organize events and festivals every day of the week. We will continue to actively promote events on Sundays.

115967.explanation: The waste collection tax with only a fixed component is unfair to small households, which are relatively more heavily taxed than large households. Charging based on the amount of waste (per bag) makes the cost distribution fairer for all households.

115912.explanation: For topics that significantly impact the local community, such as asylum reception, residents must be actively and seriously consulted. This does not necessarily have to take the form of a referendum.

115913.explanation: As far as we are concerned, this does not apply to collective facilities such as a sports hall or swimming pool. But with good mutual agreements on management quality, it could apply to smaller pieces of green space in the local neighborhood.

115915.explanation: Opening hours are less important than controlling the nuisance. As long as a horeca owner demonstrably maintains peace and order in the surroundings, he/she can be given more freedom and space to choose the times if desired. Ambacht may become a bit more fun and festive.

115917.explanation: There is not much left to be built in Ambacht, so this question seems rather irrelevant.

115918.explanation: Absolutely, preferably yesterday rather than today. If the Spreading Act is repealed, we no longer want any reception facilities. If the law remains, we are only willing to maintain the current reception on a ship.

115922.explanation: This is not a task for the municipality. Those who want to watch an organized fireworks show can already do so at two locations on that evening: the Three Rivers Point near Dordrecht and the Erasmus Bridge in Rotterdam. There, it is bigger and more beautiful.

115924.explanation: Noise standards can be agreed upon per situation and location in the permit, which is already done.

115925.explanation: Making your company accessible by law for employees and customers/guests is part of the normal tasks and costs of an entrepreneur.

115926.explanation: People with social assistance can already use various municipal regulations. Sports are not everyone's hobby, challenge, or outlet of choice, so inequality could arise. A sports club can optionally offer a moderate rate itself.

115933.explanation: Only in places where it really adds something, such as hotspots of youth nuisance.

115934.explanation: We have never received signals that there is a specific need for such a facility in our village.

115935.explanation: For their own safety and to be able to act repressively if necessary, our enforcement officers (boa's) may be equipped with more means, even if those means fall under the category of weapons.

115938.explanation: Provided we can counter large-scale 'house sluicing', a private individual should be able to buy a home, for example for a child, as a pension provision (e.g., an entrepreneur without a pension), or for personal use later in life (think of a skipper). Renting it out then is not wrong.

115939.explanation: If these objects exist and developers see potential in them, they must be funded within the developer's business case.

115941.explanation: Yes, it is a win-win situation if those who receive money also get the chance to give something back to the larger community while simultaneously gaining experience, contacts, and a sense of self-worth.

115943.explanation: We cannot answer this statement because 'receiving help with poverty' is insufficiently defined. In general, we believe that people who really need help can get it now and that should remain so.

115945.explanation: Charging points for electric cars are essentially exclusive facilities for a limited group. People with petrol cars must also remain able to park.

115946.explanation: This only makes sense in areas designed so that you cannot go faster than 30 km/h and where enforcement is also possible. If both conditions are not met, it's symbolic politics. On some roads, it is also unnecessary to enforce 30 km/h (think of Ambachtsezoom).

115947.explanation: There are no neighborhoods in Ambacht where parking problems are so severe due to parkers from outside the neighborhood. Therefore, a parking permit is not a useful measure.

115948.explanation: We are a relatively small village without a real center, and we are not burdened by heavily polluting traffic. Moreover, this falls into the category of 'bothering small entrepreneurs'.

115949.explanation: The statutory care duty lies with health insurers and care offices (except for the Social Support Act - WMO). In our view, there is already freedom of choice in care providers. To the extent that not every religion or culture is available, it is not the municipality’s task to arrange this.

115950.explanation: First, the (legal) quality criteria must be met; immediately afterward, we allow looking at the best price.

115951.explanation: We see this mainly as symbolic politics from a certain ideology. These products fall under unavoidable costs for personal, everyday care and are available in many budget variations.

115953.explanation: The tax is outdated. It is an unfair tax: only dog owners pay it, while other pets can also cause costs and nuisance. A dog is a family member, counteracts isolation, and helps promote a healthy lifestyle. Dog facilities are general provisions.

115954.explanation: We have no more room to expand business parks unless it takes place in green areas, and we do not want that.

115959.explanation: There are enough municipalities nearby where these products are available. Ambacht is not interested in a coffee shop that is almost inevitably linked to problems such as nuisance, crime, and undermining.

115962.explanation: If supply and demand meet there and when laws and regulations regarding, for example, safety are met, we see no fundamental objection to room rental.

115965.explanation: Events are already permitted on Sundays. As far as we know, no events are currently being stopped on Sundays, so more do not need to be allowed. More would be welcome, but that is up to organizers and entrepreneurs.

115967.explanation: Yes, the recycling rate combines the very worst: compulsion, much higher costs, worse service, annoyance, litter, smell, pests, and polarization. We want a source separation system for our municipality!

115912.explanation: Resident involvement is valuable. The municipal council is elected to make decisions on behalf of residents. Council members often have more information. A referendum can oversimplify complex issues. Therefore, the municipal council must make decisions.

115913.explanation: Resident initiatives are appreciated. However, managing public facilities remains a municipal task to ensure quality, accessibility, and continuity for everyone.

115915.explanation: Horeca contributes to Ambacht's vibrancy. Opening hours do require clear agreements, with attention to residents, safety, and livability. Municipal frameworks help maintain that balance.

115917.explanation: Distribution centers occupy a lot of space and cause additional traffic movements and environmental pressure. This conflicts with livability and Ambacht’s green, village-like character. Restraint in new establishments better fits the scale of the municipality.

115918.explanation: Asylum policy is determined nationally. As long as it is necessary, Ambacht takes its responsibility. When someone is in need, Ambacht receives people decently, with attention to support, guidance, and perspective.

115922.explanation: A central fireworks show can contribute to community spirit and safety during New Year's Eve. It offers a shared moment for residents while simultaneously limiting the nuisance and risks of individual fireworks.

115924.explanation: Events provide opportunities for social interaction and liveliness in Ambacht. The current noise standards already protect nearby residents. Stricter standards are therefore unnecessary, as long as careful consideration is applied.

115925.explanation: Accessibility for people with disabilities is important. At the same time, the primary responsibility lies with the entrepreneurs themselves. The municipality can support with advice and encouraging policies, without providing standard financial contributions.

115926.explanation: Sports and physical activity contribute to health, vitality, and social contacts. CDA Ambacht values investing more in sports participation for everyone. By lowering barriers, both within clubs and beyond, everyone can continue to participate regardless of income.

115933.explanation: Camera surveillance can be a valuable supplement in maintaining public order. If used carefully and purposefully, it contributes to prevention and safety, with ongoing attention to privacy.

115934.explanation: A women's shelter offers a safe place for women and children affected by domestic violence. Early shelter and nearby support help prevent worsening situations and provide perspective on recovery. There must be demonstrable demand for such a facility.

115935.explanation: Special enforcement officers (BOAs) must be able to perform their work safely and with authority. A baton can contribute to their personal safety in tense situations, provided it is used carefully and combined with proper training and clear rules.

115938.explanation: A self-occupancy obligation helps keep homes available for people who want to live in Ambacht. This keeps the housing market more accessible for first-time buyers and families.

115939.explanation: Converting vacant offices into homes offers opportunities to create additional housing more quickly. Subsidies can help get these projects off the ground. It is important to consider where support is truly needed, given the investment capacity within the real estate sector.

115941.explanation: A social community contribution adapted to someone's capabilities can help people stay active, provide structure, and remain involved in society. However, it must not lead to additional pressure or harm mental health, which would make participation more difficult.

115943.explanation: Poverty reduction requires tailored solutions. Raising income thresholds is not always effective. Targeted support and early assistance ensure resources reach those who truly need them.

115945.explanation: More and more residents are driving electric vehicles. This does not necessarily require more charging points but smarter use of existing ones. Clear agreements, such as moving cars after charging, keep spots better available for everyone.

115946.explanation: CDA Ambacht considers traffic safety very important. We see opportunities to increase the use of the 30 km/h speed limit but not everywhere in the built-up area. Customized solutions remain necessary to balance safety and traffic flow.

115947.explanation: Parking pressure varies greatly by neighborhood. A general increase in parking permits is therefore undesirable. Tailored approaches per neighborhood and situation offer more room to fairly address both residents and visitors.

115948.explanation: An environmental zone does not suit the village character of Ambacht well. Such a measure can disproportionately affect residents and entrepreneurs, while the environmental gain is limited within a municipality of this size.

115949.explanation: Good care aligns with the person behind the care request. The option to choose a care provider that fits someone's religion, culture, or beliefs contributes to trust and effectiveness of care. This applies within what is feasible and viable.

115950.explanation: When selecting youth care organizations, quality and expertise must come first. Focusing solely on price does not do justice to the complexity of care and the interests of children and families.

115951.explanation: It is important that people with financial worries receive support. This is best done through existing schemes and tailored assistance so that help reaches those who truly need it.

115953.explanation: CDA Ambacht stands for fair charges. We believe dog owners should not pay more than necessary. The dog tax has been reduced to a cost-covering level. We will continue to weigh the policy carefully.

115954.explanation: Space in Ambacht is limited. The development of Ambachtsezoom already added a large business park. Additionally, there is still vacancy on existing sites. Further expansion of business parks is therefore unnecessary and comes at the expense of greenery and livability.

115959.explanation: The use and sale of soft drugs is legally permitted, including in Ambacht. CDA Ambacht emphasizes the personal responsibility of users and entrepreneurs within clear and agreed frameworks.

115962.explanation: CDA Ambacht advocates for Room for Housing. Renting out single rooms can help better utilize existing homes. This does require clear rules to preserve neighborhood livability and prevent nuisance.

115965.explanation: Events on Sundays can foster social interaction and liveliness in Ambacht. It is important to respect quietness, support, and the surroundings so that events fit the character of the municipality.

115967.explanation: Larger households generally produce more waste than single-person households. A fixed fee per household does not account for this. Charging per bag better reflects this and encourages more conscious waste management.

115912.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports involving residents in important matters through participation, allowing them to express their wishes and concerns. Crucial is the feedback indicating why something is or is not implemented.

115913.explanation: Maintenance of a public green area can be well delegated to residents, as has been done in Thorbeckestraat. Managing a sports hall is different due to liability and government requirements.

115915.explanation: Entrepreneurs can submit requests to the municipality for their opening hours. The condition is that these hours do not cause nuisance to nearby residents.

115917.explanation: New land in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht is scarce and needed for housing. Expansion on existing business parks is possible, provided sufficient measures are taken to limit heat stress, such as green roofs.

115918.explanation: Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht is bound by the Distribution Act, through which the government requires us to accommodate a certain number of asylum seekers. This currently happens on a boat near the Karwei. Our preference is to continue this arrangement as long as the Distribution Act remains in force.

115922.explanation: First, it must be investigated whether there is demand. This could possibly be organized similarly to King's Day or in consultation with surrounding municipalities. GroenLinks-PvdA does support enforcement regarding possession and setting off fireworks.

115924.explanation: Maximum noise limits for festivals and parties are governed by legal rules. As soon as the volume does not exceed these limits, no intervention is necessary. However, organizers could provide earplugs for those who want them.

115925.explanation: Entrepreneurs whose buildings do not meet accessibility requirements can request help from the municipality, either organizational or financial. In new construction and renovation, accessibility must meet legal requirements upon completion.

115926.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that residents with a minimum income have the right to discounts or exemption from sports club membership fees.

115933.explanation: To increase the sense of safety, GroenLinks-PvdA favors more camera surveillance in locations where it is currently needed. In case of an emergency, immediate intervention must also be possible.

115934.explanation: A safe environment for women affected by domestic violence is important. A women's shelter can help with this. In Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht, there is already cooperation with 'Safe at Home', an organization where people can turn to in cases of domestic violence or child or elder abuse.

115935.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes BOAs themselves can best indicate when they consider carrying a baton necessary. In such cases, we agree immediately. Currently, efforts should focus on good collaboration between BOAs, youth workers, and street coaches.

115938.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes buyers should live in the homes they purchase. This is better for the neighborhood they live in and also prevents homes from being rented out at exorbitant prices.

115939.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes creative solutions are needed for housing, and transforming vacant office buildings into homes can be an option. The municipality can provide subsidies, but there are also national subsidies available.

115941.explanation: People receive social assistance benefits for specific reasons, and not everyone is able to work. However, people can be motivated to perform socially valuable work.

115943.explanation: Residents who earn just above the threshold to receive benefits often face more financial difficulties than those who do receive benefits. This group should also have access to assistance if they request it.

115945.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants the municipality to designate more parking spaces with charging stations for electric cars where there is demand, and only in places where they are needed.

115946.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA supports lowering the maximum speed to 30 km/h in residential neighborhoods. On the through roads in our municipality, the speed should remain 50 km/h to ensure traffic flow.

115947.explanation: All of Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht must remain accessible to its residents, and with the introduction of parking permits in certain neighborhoods, this is no longer the case. However, it is possible to explore introducing parking discs in more locations to discourage long-term parking.

115948.explanation: Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht ranks in the top 10 most polluted municipalities in the Netherlands, partly because we are squeezed between the A15 and A16 highways and the river Noord. By barring polluting vehicles from the center areas, we promote livability.

115949.explanation: Where possible and feasible, residents should be able to choose the care provider that fits their faith, culture, or beliefs.

115950.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that especially in youth assistance, quality is more important than price. Youth care can also be provided at an early stage and can prevent the need for more expensive intervention later.

115951.explanation: There must never be absenteeism among (young) women at school or work because they cannot afford tampons or sanitary pads.

115953.explanation: Three years ago, Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht was the most expensive municipality. Thanks in part to our efforts, the dog tax has been lowered to reflect the actual costs for maintenance and facilities. The dog tax should not exceed the real costs and we consider the current rate reasonable.

115954.explanation: The currently available land in our municipality should be designated for housing and green spaces. Companies may expand on existing industrial sites, but only if they also invest in environmental measures and greenery to reduce heat stress.

115959.explanation: Establishing a coffeeshop could lower the threshold for our youth to start using drugs. Also, a coffeeshop can cause nuisance in the form of odor for the surroundings and create a feeling of insecurity.

115962.explanation: This possibility of allocating rooms should be investigated. The condition is that there must be strict oversight to prevent exploitation of labor migrants.

115965.explanation: Events are important because they connect people and strengthen a sense of community, and they provide an escape from the daily routine. Allowing more events on Sundays also gives people who have to work on other days the opportunity to participate.

115967.explanation: We voted against the introduction of this system, but from experience know that this method of waste collection is the cheapest. For people with babies, a temporary arrangement should be made to allow more bags. We want to make the costs of each collection method transparent.

115912.explanation: We support advisory referendums in applicable cases.

115913.explanation: If the residents want it and it improves quality, then it is definitely worth considering.

115915.explanation: Certainly, we must support entrepreneurs in this, provided it does not cause too much nuisance.

115917.explanation: It generates too little employment and leads to a lot of traffic movements.

115918.explanation: We must comply with the Distribution Act. Moreover, asylum and displaced persons accommodations on boats have so far caused little nuisance.

115922.explanation: The residents have the right to receive a nice municipal show funded by their taxes once a year.

115924.explanation: There are already legal standards for that, which we consider sufficient.

115925.explanation: That is the responsibility of the entrepreneur themselves.

115926.explanation: That does not necessarily have to be with the municipality. Perhaps in the form of special assistance or by using foundations set up for this purpose.

115933.explanation: Only in places with structural nuisance and it should not be permanent. Evaluation must always take place after a few years.

115934.explanation: We do not currently know if this need exists. We have not yet received such signals, but if there is a need, we are certainly in favor.

115935.explanation: Other instruments are possible. Proportionality should be considered.

115938.explanation: Unless it was already an investment property. Protection of property rights.

115939.explanation: Project developers should pay for that themselves.

115941.explanation: A social contribution may be required.

115943.explanation: Only if the limit is too low can it be raised.

115945.explanation: Certainly, if it appears that there is a need, it should be encouraged. Also, possibilities for charging on private property should be explored.

115947.explanation: There should be fewer rules instead.

115948.explanation: First conduct research into the impact on residents and entrepreneurs of Ambacht.

115949.explanation: We are in favor of inclusivity and can imagine that people want that. But there must be enough care providers for that.

115951.explanation: That is not a task of the municipality.

115953.explanation: A long-standing wish of D66 since 2018.

115954.explanation: We have sufficient business parks and no more space for new ones.

115962.explanation: In light of the housing shortage, every opportunity must be seized.

115965.explanation: The events policy should not block initiatives on Sundays.

115967.explanation: The costs of post-separation are more expensive than the current method we use for collecting waste/materials.

115912.explanation: Only on issues where there is a lot of disagreement.

115913.explanation: If one wants that, it should be possible.

115915.explanation: A municipality is also about the function of equal treatment and protection of citizens.

115918.explanation: We must comply with the Dispersion Act.

115922.explanation: To prevent fireworks throughout the entire municipality.

115924.explanation: Should be possible from time to time.

115933.explanation: Only when this is meaningful from a protection standpoint.

115934.explanation: There has been no need indicated for this yet.

115935.explanation: Only if it turns out to be necessary for their own safety.

115938.explanation: Sometimes a son or daughter needs to be helped.

115939.explanation: That should be possible if funds are available for it.

115941.explanation: This is the standard. If someone cannot do it (physically or mentally), an exception can be made.

115943.explanation: This is already happening in the various Drechtsteden municipalities.

115945.explanation: Only if this does not come at the expense of existing parking spaces.

115946.explanation: Yes, for the inner streets, but not for the exit routes to other places or highways.

115947.explanation: Parking permits are not something for our municipality.

115948.explanation: This municipality is not there yet. For the time being, this will not happen. Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht is more of a residential municipality in the center.

115949.explanation: For us, it does not matter what religion the care provider has. It is about the care this person delivers.

115950.explanation: In Ambacht we already have a special organization, Hi5Ambacht, that coordinates this. It is subsidized by the municipality, but price does not need to be the primary focus. The initial priority is the help provided.

115953.explanation: We have held this view for years.

115954.explanation: When there is still space available.

115959.explanation: There are enough in other municipalities. No need for it here.

115962.explanation: This does not apply to the landlords.

115965.explanation: If they come forward and request a permit.

115967.explanation: This only happens with waste in the gray container, where the first six bags are free. So waste must be better sorted as a result.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more events on Sundays.

titleParts.glossary: A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The residents of Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht should be able to express their opinions on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: A domestic violence shelter (women's refuge) should be established in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
There should be more CCTV surveillance in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who patrol for safety in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht should be equipped with a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente worden omgebouwd tot woningen.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht should introduce parking permits in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Business parks in Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht should be allowed to expand.
Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht should introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a new coffeeshop (cannabis coffee shop).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should stop the system in which citizens pay per bag to dispose of their waste.
